For this was the other method by means of which the “soul” entered mankind. The mysterious rhythms which man had caught from Nature were imitated in the movements of his own limbs. Thus was it that man felt himself at one with Nature and with the Powers that ruled her. —Steiner, GA 11
The transition to single-sexedness was guided by advanced spiritual entities. As mentioned previously, in every age, there are those who are “more” advanced, those that lag behind, and those in the Goldilocks zone of development. Likewise, there were superhuman beings who previously inhabited the hermaphroditic forms. These beings are termed the “Arhats”, Sons of the Fire Nebula. They are the most advanced Pitris (Lunar Fathers). Their consciousness evolved on a previous state of Earth’s evolution, completing their “human” stage there, becoming a kind of angelic humanity. In Lemuria, they acted as custodians of the pre-sexed humanity. After the sex division, they could no longer fully incarnate, and refused to incarnate, seeing as they require a balanced vessel. For if they incarnated into a one-sided sexed body, it would have meant a descent into a lower, “unfree” state for them (although, there are exceptions called the “Great Hermaphrodites”: the highest Arhats who did momentarily incarnate at the cusp of the transition to guide the process directly. They possessed the full, balanced power of the ancient hermaphroditic form, and were seen as divine messengers). Because of this, they worked from the spiritual world to implant sexual love and the impulse for reproduction as a sacrificial service in order that it continue humanity. They regulated sexual life. A less advanced group of Lunar Pitris, or beings who had undergone a different evolutionary path, were also presented. They acted as the bridge between the spiritual guides and the developing humanity. Unlike the Arhats who were not receptive to sensory impressions, these beings were receptive to both spiritual impressions and sensory impressions. Their task was to attract human consciousness toward the external, sensory world, enabling the development of sense-based perception and free will.
Brief note on the Moon: the recap above would be incomplete if it did not account for Lucifer, the Luciferic Spirits, and the moon. I will only briefly touch on these, seeing as we do not want to get too bogged down in the details. Previously, it was said that the Earth “hardened”. The stimulus for this was the departure of the Moon from the Earth, leaving a scar now characterized by the Ring of Fire. Before the Moon separated in the middle of the Lemurian Epoch, the description of Earth as provided held. It was the Moon’s exit that caused the Earth to harden to its current mineralistic state. This separation initiated the Earth’s rotation, creating the rhythms of day and night and the seasonal year. It can be said that the departure of the moon established the primary duality and polarity in the natural world into which the now dual-human form (male and female) would incarnate. The Earth became “cradled in between Sun and Moon influences”. The departing moon took with it the “terrible instincts” and “all the evil influences” that had developed in humanity’s astral body during a prior hellish phase. This purification allowed a fresh start for beings capable of higher evolution on the now-hardened Earth. The aforementioned “terrible instincts” and “evil influences” that had developed in man did not cease to be. They became associated with the moon as a separate cosmic body. From this new position, their influence could on now work upon the Earth and humanity from the outside, rather than being mixed indistinguishably from within. Soon after the Moon’s departure, the Elohim, or Jehovah beings, performed a great sacrifice to advance human evolution. Their act was to infuse the germ of the human “I”, or ego, into the human astral body. This was a slow, gradual process of “rippling in” the I-consciousness, creating the previously talked about new interiority. The “backwards spirits” from the Old Moon (the Luciferic beings) saw in this process a chance: having not completed their own evolution on the old moon, they found a new field for their development. So, they intercepted the process. As the Elohim poured the I-substance into the human astral body, the Luciferic beings rushed into that same astral body from the “other side”, attempting to merge with and take possession of this newly forming center of human consciousness. They blended their own attributes of brilliant intellect, fiery passion for independence, and a love of “freedom”, directly into the seat of human desires and emotions. “How can this be a bad thing?”, the Luciferic among you might ask. Well, it is a double-edged sword. The Elohim, or Jehovahic influence gave us lawful form and the foundational “I”, the capacity for reverence, and so on and so forth. But it also risks a humanity that would have developed in a state of innocence, with a childlike obedience, lacking true individuality and the capacity for self-chosen good. Likewise, the Luciferic influence (Backward Moon Spirits) gave us self-consciousness, artistic inspiration, intellectual brilliance, and the drive for independence. Their (or his, both are valid) “infection” brought along with it passionate desire, pride, rebellion, the ability to lie (there is no use lying if the mode of communication used in the past was a kind of telepathy, and there was no compulsion to lie since an interiority in the past wasn’t developed), and the temptation to use freedom selfishly against the divine laws. This union of the two influences, along with the departure of the moon, and descent of the world and man into matter, is the Biblical “Fall”. We have before us an astrological schema, wherein the Moon’s physical separation created the setup for the battle over the soul of man. The Sun remained the center of the Christ impulse, the Moon became the externalized base for the Luciferic influence, and the Earth became the arena where the human “I” caught between these influences, must exercise its freedom to choose its path. This “infection” still affects us today, manifest in specific traits. However, enough have been said on this topic.
If the Lemurian epoch (at least, starting in the middle of it) is characterized by a more “masculine” mode of interface- that of powerful will and mastery over the body and natural forces, then over time this mode would start to develop a more “feminine” bias towards highly developed memory. Therefore, this change, this metamorphosis of consciousness, physicality, and civilization marks a definitive step away from an instinctual union with nature, towards a more self-aware historically grounded existence. This new epoch is termed the “Atlantean”. Lemurian consciousness was a dreamlike picture-consciousness, where powerful will and direct perception of nature reigned, but it also lacked memory. No memory existed at this time to retain ideas/concepts or experiences. For this reason, the transitory state from the middle-Lemurian period, into the Atlantean found women with a specialized role. The education of girls was sharply differentiated from the “hardening” of boys, since it aimed at developing a specific soul quality. Girls were deliberately exposed to powerful natural phenomena and brutal fights between men. The intention wasn’t to provoke fear or aggression, but to allow them to perceive these events with calmness and feel an admiration for the display of strength and prowess, in an effort to foster a disposition to dreaming, to “revelling in fancy”. So, through their senses, women drew in the forces of nature, and allowed them to react upon their souls. This process formed “the germs of memory” as Steiner calls it, which to be clear was a monumental step in the development of human consciousness. True memory did not exist in humanity before this. Thus, the Atlantean awoke into a world of memory. While they did not yet possess our logical and abstract reason, they thought in vivid, remembered images and sense impressions. This newfound ability to recall the past gave their lives a sense of personal history and continuity, which became the very foundation of their societies. Perhaps this is what Plato meant when he said that all learning is remembering? He was, after all, an initiate of the Pythagorean mystery school. Although one can argue it was Socrates who said this, you can never tell if it’s Socrates speaking or Plato speaking through Socrates at first glance!.
Let us clairvoyantly observe the old continent of Atlantis, which must be sought where the Atlantic Ocean now lies, between Africa and Europe on the one side, and America on the other. This continent was encircled by a sort of warm stream, a stream about which clairvoyant consciousness reveals that, strange as it may sound, it flowed upwards from the South, through Baffins Bay, towards the north of Greenland, encircling it and then, flowing over to the East, gradually cooled down; then, at a time when Siberia and Russia had not yet risen to the surface, it flowed down near the Ural mountains, turned, touched the Eastern Carpathians, flowed into the region occupied by the present Sahara, and finally streamed towards the Atlantic Ocean near the Bay of Biscay; so that it flowed in a perfectly unbroken stream. You will understand that only the remnants of this stream still remain. This is the Gulf Stream, which at that time encircled the Atlantean Continent. You will now also understand that, with the Greeks, the life of the soul is remembrance. The picture of Oceanos arose in them, which is a memory of that Atlantean epoch. Their picture of the world is not so very incorrect, because it was drawn from the old Atlantean epoch. The stream that came down by Spitzbergen as a warm current, and gradually cooled and so on, — the region encircled by this stream the Chinese have literally reproduced by enclosing within their Great Wall the culture which they rescued from the Atlantean epoch. There was as yet no history in the Atlantean civilization, hence the Chinese civilization is also in some ways lacking in history. Thus we have there something pre-Indian, something coming from Atlantis. —Steiner, GA 121
Trust and authority was suited towards those with the longest experience, for they had the most “knowledge” to draw upon. Hence, education was focused on imprinting life’s images upon the student for future recall. This cognitive evolution was mirrored by a change in man’s physical environment and technological mastery, a topic we will not discuss in detail here. By physical environment, it is important to emphasize a change in the world. The Moon’s separation triggered the final hardening of Earth and it’s atmosphere. Earth, now hardened during the Atlantean epoch, had a much denser atmosphere, much “thinner” water, enabling Atlantean technology (e.g., vehicles that floated above the ground). Where the Lemurian was a “born magician” (as Steiner says) who shaped soft matter through will, the Atlantean became a bio-technician. They learned to harness the dormant life force within plant seeds, transforming this energy into technical power for vehicles that floated above the ground, representing a civilization built on a learned (albeit still image-based) manipulation of nature’s subtler forces. The fabric of human interaction was rewoven. The Lemurian wordless “thought-reading” gave way to the development of true language in Atlantis. For the first time, experiences could be communicated, names given to things, and a social substance was created through speech. Social structures also transformed. While both epochs were led by spiritual initiates, Atlantean society at large became organized around precedent and remembered custom, leading to a stable, but slow paced civilization deeply allied with nature. Even buildings reflected this reality, with people living in garden-like settlements, and houses made of plants (with their forms directed by the will of man). Ultimately, the transition between the Lemurian and Atlantean epochs was an evolutionary bottleneck. The great majority of Lemurian humanity, unable to develop memory, declined into stunted forms. The Atlanteans emerged solely from a small, advanced segment of late Lemuria, capable of taking this step in soul development.
We should be permitted to describe the Atlantean epoch in relation to our investigation on male and female. The Atlanteans inherited the spiritual duality/polarity that emerged from the Lemurian sex division. The masculine principle remained aligned with will and external power. In Lemuria, this was expressed as mastery of natural forces and physical hardening. In Atlantis, this translated into the application of will through technology, particularly the harnessing of life force for vehicles and machinery, and the physical governance of communities. The feminine principle remained aligned with imagination, memory, and internal wisdom. The “germs of memory” cultivated by Lemurian women became the foundation of all Atlantean civilization. I spoke earlier prematurely on the origin of good and evil without clarification, this is where it should rightfully be invoked. Because they held image-based consciousness, the capacity for forming the first moral concepts of Good and Evil, and deep connection to nature’s inner rhythms became the soul of Atlantean culture.
Likewise, their social life shows a clear divergence stemming from these principles, specifically on the topic of the concept of property. An Atlantean’s ingenuity created for him the concept of private property. The power to externally shape, build, and own the products of technical skill and intellect belong to the masculine domain, aligning with the will principle. On the other hand, nature is common to all men, therefore it belongs to the feminine domain. What was built upon nature- the garden-like settlements with houses made of intertwined trees- was considered common property. Here is a reflection of the feminine principle of alliance with and the interpretation of nature as a shared heritage. The invocation of private property and public ownership does not stand solely as an economic, or sociological, designation. What followed forth from Atlantean spirit was an ontological reality which influenced how early post-Atlantean societies understood, or took in, the cosmos, divinity, and social orders. What became expressed as the cultural and ritual expressions of this worldview are known as the goddess religions today. However, in Atlantean times, “religion” did not warrant a separate activity, seeing as it was the pervasive medium in which they lived. It would be much more accurate to say that from their very being emerged a theocratic mode of life, with the centrality of life for Atlantean culture revolving around the Temples of the Mysteries. These temples were the seats of the initiates, whose wisdom was received through supersensible perception (a faculty more akin to the imaginative clairvoyance developed by Lemurian women). Thus, the highest principle guiding Atlantis was the impersonal, cosmic feminine force, often termed “The Goddess of Necessity” or “Ananke”. This goddess personified the unbreakable laws of the cosmos, the divine “wisdom” that flows through nature and destiny. Atlanteans did not “believe in” Gods, they experienced and submitted themselves to these laws, as much as Lemurian women experienced the forces of nature.
You can find the remnants of this culture in transformed, but recognizable ways, within the Neolithic and Bronze Age goddess culture of the post-Atlantean world in the forms of Old Europe, Anatolia, Minoan Crete, and early Mesopotamia. After the flood, yes that flood, and the dispersal of Atlantean survivors, this abstract principle often crystallized into the worship of a particularized, personified Earth Goddess. Look to the function, where the goddess now personally embodies what was once an impersonal principle. She was the owner of all natural bounty: the fields, waters, wildlife, even fertility itself. The land was still considered her common property, and humanity’s right to use it was seen as a gift from her, requiring reciprocity through ritual and respect. This, of course, stands in stark contrast to concepts of private, kingly, or state-owned land that emerged with later patriarchal, Indo-European, and urbanized cultures; a shift that aligns with the rising masculine principle of demarcated ownership and conquest. Archeology of early goddess worshiping societies reveal social structures that echo Atlantean description. In sites like Çatalhöyük (7500 BCE) there is little evidence of personal hoarding. Homes were all similar in size, and symbolic wealth, such as ritual items, were focused on shrines. This suggests a community-centric economy. Law was given from the goddess, in distinction to decree by a sky-god king. It flowed from the nature cycles embodied by her. Social harmony depended on aligning with her rhythms (like the cultivation of plants, harvest, lunar cycles). There exists an architectural legacy as well. The Atlantean settlements built with nature evolved into the cave sanctuaries and later temple complexes of goddess religions. Places like the Hypogeum of Ħal-Saflieni or the Minoan peak sanctuaries are now recontextualized as ritualized natural spaces where the nurturing power of the Earth (read: the feminine principle) was accessed. Cyclical vs. Linear time is another distinction, however, let’s not get too bogged down in the details.
One final, and important, thing worth noting is a continuation on the idea of “fructification”, or reproduction. The gradual descent of human consciousness into the physical process of reproduction marks the slow, epoch-spanning shift, from a spiritual, unconscious conception to a conscious, physical one. As established earlier, early Lemurian humanity was hermaphroditic, capable of self-generation. This was not a physical act. It was an etheric/spiritual process where the creative life force was directed inwardly or outwardly with full conscious mastery by spiritual beings. Man’s consciousness being dreamlike found “fructification” an act of higher spiritual entities working through the human form. This is a “virginal” state. It is a purity derived from the lack of conscious, sensual involvement in a physical, reproductive act. Following the separation of the sexes, propagation required the union of male and female, but the human soul’s consciousness was not yet fully incarnated into the physical body and its process. The state of consciousness of the Atlantean based the sentient soul as dominant. The intellectual mind (the “I”, or ego) was still weakly developed, and therefore the physical act of procreation occurred in a trance-like, or dream state, not fully comprehended and owned by the participants. There was no: I am having sex in order to have a kid, because there wasn’t an I. Conception is then a spiritual event, not a biological one. The common, unquestioned understanding was that “the children of men were sent by the Gods”. A spiritual being would descend to incarnate, and the physical union served as an early portal for this event. This is the true meaning of “immaculate conception”, a conception untainted by personal desire or conscious physicality, perceived as a pure act of divine service. As the epoch progressed, the “I”-consciousness began to stir. Steiner notes that in later Atlantean times, what happened “began to be experienced in the imagination, but this was subjectively colored”. The first glimmers of personal, sensory awareness of the act appeared, clouding old spiritual clarity.
Where the Atlantean lives in guided will/consciousness, with their will being directed externally by divine messengers and spiritual laws being perceptible in nature, they only acted from a kind of instinct and vaguely sensed divine governance. The current epoch we are in, the fifth so far, reverses the demand by inauguration of Manu, who prompted a self-directed/self-determined consciousness through the faculty of thought. Manu’s key commandment was to venerate an invisible God, a deity of whom no sensory-visible image could resemble. From here, the shift from external image to internally grasped concept commenced. Humanity could no longer rely on external spiritual forces or visible leaders alone. They had to form concepts internally of the divine. What this means for morality is that it’s task is to find itself as directed freely by its own thoughts. It must voluntarily place those thoughts at the service of higher powers. The old Lemurian and Atlantean gender principles did not disappear. They were thrust into this new environment of freedom where they had, or rather have, to be consciously mastered and spiritualized. The masculine principle of activity expresses itself in this current epoch, named the “Fifth Post-Atlantean” epoch (or the “Aryan” epoch, but this has a negative political connotation), as… well, it hasn’t fully expressed itself. The challenge, as guided by free thought, is to use thought and will as a tool for executing divine principles understood inwardly. The risk is materialistic, selfish intellect (via the Ahrimanic and Luciferic forces respectively). The feminine principle has as its goal the usage of imaginative and memory-based faculties consciously to transform personal feeling into a vessel for understanding the “invisible” spiritual world. The risk is subjectivity and sentimentality. Either way, both principles are to be sanctified through free choice. Masculine intellect must learn reverence, and feminine imagination must seek spiritual truth, because their union is no longer an automatic function of nature. It must be achieved consciously.
The complimentary duality of male and female because the proving grounds for this epoch’s spiritual task. By working to harmonize the thinking will (masculine) with the moral imagination (feminine) within themselves and society, individuals spiritually evolve. The internal work is probably best described by the external historical development from goddess religions (which represents the unconscious feminine principle of nature) toward an ethical monotheism (representing the conscious masculine principle of unifying spiritual law).
We have before us the modern spiritual landscape, awaiting interpretation. Firstly, we can begin with scripture:
Simon Peter said to them: “Let Mary [Magdalene] go forth from among us, for women are not worthy of the life.” Jesus said: “Behold, I shall lead her, that I may make her male, in order that she also may become a living spirit like you males. For every woman who makes herself male shall enter into the kingdom of heaven.” —Gospel of Thomas, Saying 114
What is meant here might at first seem like a condemnation of women, with an implied context of modern materialism wherein woman is the physical female character, and her spiritual character stems from her biology. But given our description of the origin of the sexed distinction, and it’s telos, this quote is contextualized as a description of the necessary spiritual transformation for all humans in the Post-Atlantean epoch. What is meant by “making her male” is a metaphor for spiritual initiation. The male principle is the active, autonomous, thinking spirit. It is the capacity for individual judgment, and penetrating insight into the Logos. This is the principle humanity must develop to achieve independence. The female principle symbolizes the receptive, imaginative, soul-based wisdom of old. It is the capacity for deep feeling and moral intuition; the capacity for connection to nature’s inner being. When Jesus says he will lead Mary to “become a living spirit like you males”, he is simply indicating that the male disciples have already begun to develop this active spiritual activity. Mary, as the highly developed feminine soul, must now consciously integrate this “male” principle to achieve the full androgynous state of the living spirit.
This “conscious” state, did not emerge immediately, as you might have guessed with all epochal transitions. In fact, many people today are walking NPCs, sleepwalkers if you will. The gradual awakening of the “I” to its own physical nature was a process that took millennia. This is reflected in the myths of ancient cultures. Stories of gods impregnating human women (such as Zeus or Dionysus) or divine annunciations are cultural memories of a time when conception was genuinely perceived as an intervention from the spiritual world. As the post-Atlantean epochs unfolded, human consciousness steadily, but surely, descended into the physical body. With this came greater awareness of, and identification with, the physical-sexual nature of propagation. The “subjective coloring” previously mentioned with respect to Atlantean procreation increased. Steiner points definitively toward the 4th century of the Christian era as the point in time where the human soul had become fully incarnated. The intellectual “I” had taken firm hold of the physical brain and body. From this point onward, procreation became a fully conscious, physically understood act. The old spiritual perception of the gods sending children faded from direct human experience, becoming a matter of legend or perhaps belief, rather than perceived reality.
The myth of Nerthus presents a very apt mythological representation for this transitional state of human consciousness between Atlantis and now. As recorded by Tacitus, Nerthus was an Earth goddess worshiped by northern Germanic tribes. Her primary ritual involved a yearly procession where her sacred image, veiled on a chariot, was drawn by cows to various settlements. Wherever she arrived, there was peace and festivity. After being paraded through the fields, the chariot, the goddess, and the vestments were washed in a sacred, hidden lake. The slaves who performed this washing were then drowned in the lake. The goddess, having been cleansed, was returned to her sacred grove. The unenlightened interpretation of this story is one of a savage, and primitive fertility rite. But the more initiated among us will understand Nerthus as the “virginal” Earth soul. She represents Nature itself in its spiritual and fruitful aspect. This is the same divine, creative force that was perceived during the Atlantean “immaculate conception”. She is the ensouled earth. Her procession through the fields is an enactment of the spiritual fructification of nature, performed while man was still conscious of this process. The veiling signifies that this spiritual reality was beginning to withdraw from direct human perception. The chariot, drawn by cows (symbols of nurturing and earthly fertility) shows that this spiritual force was still connected to and working through the physical world, but was now being “carried” by it, no longer fully visible. Perhaps the most interesting aspect of the story is the purification via death. The ritual drowning symbolizes the necessary “death” of the old clairvoyant consciousness so that the new self-conscious “I” could be born. The slaves, representing the lower, bound aspects of the human soul that served the old perception, must be eliminated for the purified goddess (ideal the principle of life), in order that she returns to her grove (the hidden spiritual world). For more on this mythology, see below:
Around 3000 BC there was a major Mystery Center in Jutland (Denmark) and the temple priests decreed that in certain tribes, the sexual union of human beings must only take place during the first quarter of the year, at the time of the first full moon after the spring equinox. This resulted in the remarkable power of the physical bodies. The season of Easter was the time for conception with the view to a time of birth at Christmas. This was seen as a holy mystery of the cosmos and was later symbolized in the worship of Nerthus. This process, as part of the religious worship of the Vanir (gods) in the people (called by Tacitus) the Ingaevones, had a particularly strong experience of the process of conception through a kind of dream annunciation or ‘knowing dream-consciousness’, in which a spiritual being appeared to the one who was conceiving and announced to her the human being who was to come to the Earth through her. The godly being that appeared, Ertha or Nerthus, was perceived as male-female rather than purely female. Only later did this become wholly female. Conception was brought about in a state of unconsciousness. The woman who was conceiving was however truly conscious of the visionary appearance of a spiritual being descending from spiritual worlds to announce the coming child. These women even foresaw the face of the coming child. Furthermore: among the tribes of the Ingaevones, the first human being to be born in the holy night, was chosen to become, at the age of thirty, the leader or king for three years. He was raised and developed in specials ways in the first thirty years, and at the end of the three years underwent a kind of symbolic death. This tradition was deeply rooted in the population across Northern-Western Europe and developed a special relationship with the birth of a special child, born on what is now Christmas, who is to become king for a period of three years. The result is that the Northern stream had an unconscious feelings connection with what was a foreshadowing of the life of Jesus-Christ. This stream is referred to in Luke’s gospel as the shepards.
Where does this leave us, now and in the future? It would be best to describe our current situation. Man, being constituted by separate sexes, represents the full externalization and physicalization of the human reproductive process, characterized by the complete incarnation of the human “I” into a mineral-physical body, where the spiritual forces that guide reproduction now work upon us from the outside, most notably, from the Moon. The marker which most prominently points out the transmuted being is the relationship between the human reproductive Rhythm and the cosmic rhythm of the moon. In earlier stages (Atlantis), women’s menstrual cycle were in direct, unconscious synchrony with the lunar phases. The spiritual forces of the Moon worked within the human being’s own etheric organization. In our current epoch, these cycles have “freed themselves” from this lockstep rhythm. This liberation is a sign of individualization and the freedom of the ego, or “I”. The biological process has no longer become an instinct to be prompted externally in source, but has become part of the individual’s physical organism. In other words, during a previous planetary stage of development, the spiritual beings and forces active were our “environment”. The separation of the physical Moon from the Earth was an act of internalization and differentiation. The “soul”, or essence of that old stage of existence, was retained within the evolving human being. This internalized lunar soul principle specifically unfolded as the human sexual and reproductive forces after the Lemurian epoch. Therefore, the menstrual cycle imitates the outer rhythm of the lunar phases, but they don’t actually coincide! It is a freed, internalized copy of the cosmic rhythm. Steiner calls this relationship “the most striking example of a human-cosmic relationship that can still be observed today”. The phenomenon of menstrual synchrony is also explained along these lines. Women radiate etheric (life-force) and astral (soul) energies. In close proximity, these shared subtle bodies interact and can harmonize their rhythms. Living together provides a shared “etheric base” (like Huygens’ connected pendulums) that allows their individual cycles to attune to each other and, indirectly, to the cosmic archetype that they all share internally.
Another aspect to take note of is the development of the embryo in the womb. This development is understood as a recapitulation of cosmic and human evolution, where the physical gestation period mirrors the vast epochs of spiritual development we’ve briefly discussed. The elementary principle to understand is that the development of the individual human being (ontogeny) replays, in a condensed form, the evolution of humanity and the cosmos (cosmogeny). During the approximately nine months of gestation, the embryo passes through stages that correspond to the four planetary conditions as Old Saturn, Old Sun, Old Moon, and Earth, and later, the Lemurian, Atlantean, and Post-Atlantean epochs. Do not mistake this for a physical resemblance, this is a morphological and force-based recapitulation. The formative forces that shaped humanity over eons are active in a condensed sequence to shape the individual human body. In the context of our current bisexual mode of procreation, the embryonic process highlights the complexity of the physical act. When a human embryo is conceived today, it is the starting point for an entire cosmic human evolutionary journey to be replayed. The physical bodies of the parents carry the condensed spiritual history of all past stages, which is activated and sequenced in the new being. The creative forces are fully externalized and operate through natural law, and as such, the intelligent process of morphogenesis (where a single cell becomes a complex human) is the visible manifestation of spiritual formative forces working in perfect, lawful sequence. Humanity witnesses this mystery from the outside, but has largely lost the conscious, internal grasping of the guiding spiritual beings that ancient myths described. This entire gestational recapitulation really serves one ultimate purpose. This purpose is in preparing a physical instrument refined enough to receive and express an individual, self-conscious human spirit in the form of the “I”. This is essentially the significance of the embryo in the womb, it is physical proof and consequence of our entire story.
In the future, as early as the year 8000 AD., the physical constitution of women will change, leading to widespread and eventual universal sterility under current biological systems. This will be triggered by the return of the Moon. However, it is much too early (or rather too late?) to worry about this. As human spiritual maturity advances, physical evolution ceases. When the physical body completes its development around the age of sexual maturity and evolves no further, the necessity for physical propagation through birth will end. That is all to be said on the topic.
The Story Understood Well, that was quite the narrative! Unless you didn’t read all the way through and skipped to this section, in which case, shame on you. Otherwise, what follows next is the question of what we can extract from this schema about the structure of man and woman. Even when taken metaphorically, what has been presented can provide us insights about the nature of masculinity, femininity, and human consciousness. What this “story” essentially conveys is that there is a necessary structural manifestation of a journey from unconscious unity, through differentiation, towards a future synthesis. The power lies in explaining why we might experience the tensions of being and duality as we do. As we developed self-awareness and an interior world, we became identified more and more with our physical bodies and senses. This shifted our mode of engagement with the world from intuitive connection to sensory observation. Love, previously an unconscious spiritual force of unity, become channeled through the physical and emotional body. It became sensual, tied to desire, personal attraction, selfishness, and the physical act. This is not inherently negative, for if these did not exist, then any necessary ground of freedom would not truly be free. Human love is a turbulent mix of higher aspiration and lower desire, it is the act of love itself learning to become conscious through man. In the above, man’s physiology became warm-blooded. The hardening of Earth and specialization of sexes parallels a shift in life’s very principle. The warm blood state contrasts with the “cold” and malleable state of primordial androgyny. The insight here is that to be “warm-blooded” is to be self-sustaining, but also insulated, and individuated. This symbolically represents the development of a constant internal state separate from the environment. This is the physiological corollary to the birth of the ego. The soft, plastic Lemurian was environmentally embedded, whereas the warm-blooded human maintains an independent interiority. This independence also stands as a prerequisite for freedom, but also for alienation from nature.
Finally, there is the fact that man already is implicitly both female and male. From one perspective, the “I” is genderless, and incarnation during the previous few epochs provides a cross-gender soul experience. The physical form did not bifurcate randomly into male and female. It was driven by a specific, directional pressure, conditioned by the hardening of matter. As the Earth’s substance condensed, the previously plastic human body could no longer serve as the neutral medium for the androgynous soul. Instead, it was forced into specialization, in a manner that created a deliberate inversion between the soul’s essence and the body’s function. Consequently, the female body specialized as the necessary vessel for externalizing the Will (the masculine principle). The active, shaping force of the soul’s male aspect needed a physical instrument to engage the newly resistant world. The female forme was fashioned into the appropriate conduit for manifesting power and will directly into nature and society. This is why, in the old epoch, women stood as the priestesses. Their bodies were instruments through which cosmic will could be channeled. So paradoxically, the biological female became the physical home for the active, masculine principle of Will in its outgoing expression. However, her enduring “core”, the formative ground of her consciousness, remained rooted in the imaginative, feminine aspect (Kriya Shakti) from which she originated. The other side of the coin has the male body specializing as the vessel for internalizing Imagination (the feminine principle). With the loss of the old dreamlike consciousness, the soul’s female aspect could no longer project itself freely onto a malleable world. It required a new, protected, interior space. The male body then became the locus for this forced internalization. The rigorous training in “hardening” and endurance suppressed the external, sensual expression of will (which was now more the domain of the female form) and thereby dam up the creative, formative energy. This dammed-up, inward (and upward) rushing force was the substance that built the interiority, the refined nervous system, and the physical brain. Thought is the off spring of this internalized imagination, it is Kriya Shakti turned inward on itself, generating abstract ideation. Therefore, the biological male became the home for the internalized, formative principle of imagination (the feminine principle). Yet, just like the female, his foundational soul principle remained the Will (Iccha Shakti), the force he was tasked to apply from within. There is a structural cross-polarization here. The female possesses a body specialized as an instrument of externalized Will, while her soul is at core Imagination. The male possesses a body specialized as an instrument of internalized imagination (the thinking organism) while his soul is at core Will. There is a structural tension present here, where the soul calls from one pole, but the body is tooled for its opposite. Between these two ends is the makings of an individual consciousness, and a telos geared towards holistic integration.
From another perspective, this fact of “man in woman and woman in man” is almost common sense. Fertilization by the Spirit (the previously mentioned process of the inward turning of creative energy which created our capacity for thought) makes us whole, insofar as we seek a conscious integration of the other polarity, finding its correlate in the spiritual task of becoming whole. However, all pretenses to “being a man” and “being a woman” are somewhat trivialized and revealed as superficial performances once one recognizes that has never been a “100%” feminine or masculine. This is evident on multiple levels. Biologically, each human being is a genetic mosaic of male and female potentiality. Physiologically, both sexes produce “opposite” hormones (testosterone in women, estrogen in men), of course in quantities essential to health and equilibrium. We are, in a way, walking polarities. This inherent bisexuality extends into the realm of character as well. There exists no pure “manly” will utterly devoid of receptive imagination, nor a purely “feminine” imagination completely free of directive will. The most decisive leader requires an intuitive empathy to guide them effectively, and even the most nurturing caregiver must summon formidable will in order to protect and provide. What we label as masculine or feminine traits are temporary accents of one pole. As of now, this narrative, or at the very least the insights gained from it, does not invent a duality, but it does provide a teleological explanation for it. Our inherent mixed state is the direct result and proof of the old split. Consequently, rigid gender roles and identities are revealed as evolutionary scaffold. I can see the argument made for them, perhaps for a time, to cultivate the specialized strengths of each pole, but ultimately they are meant to be outgrown. In fact, the moment they are to be outgrown is the moment they are grasped in their totality inwardly. The spiritual task of the current epoch is precisely beyond hinging individual “I” identity as a man or a woman, in any exclusive sense of the label. It is in recognizing ourselves as conscious Spirits learning to marry both principles within.
What follows below are various notes related to the topic of sexuality that I hope can serve one well.
Gender What is gender? Is it a socially mediated reality? Is it something irreducible to a biological given? The question of gender can only be answered after having answered the question of sex and sexual differentiation. Sex refers to the biological differentiation into male and female physical bodies. It is a phenomenon of the current Earth epoch, specifically arising from the middle of the Lemurian period onward. We find its origin as the result of the “hardening” of matter. As the Earth’s substance condensed, the originally androgynous, plastic human body could no longer express the fully bipolar soul. It split into two specialized physical forms. Sex is a temporary, necessary sacrifice for the sake of the evolution of man. The male and female bodies are very specialized instruments, each optimized for a specific cosmic, or global, function. The female body is structured to channel forces outward into the world, where the male body is an instrument for internalizing the Imagination principle. It is structured to dam up creative spiritual force and turn it inward to build up the brain and intellectual capacity. Sex, insofar as it is a biological reality, has two important aspects to its reproduction. As it relates to the reproduction of the “self”, it represents further breaks from external cosmic forces and beings and takes a more independent posture for itself. Seeing as we are not fully independent, we are still linked to the Earth as of now. As it relates to the reproduction of the being in the form of population, the two seemingly counterposed bodies require each other in order to make another. Physical sexuality is a transitory stage. It will eventually be transcended as humanity spiritualizes, with future stages involving the return of a form of spiritual androgyny (the Moon’s return leading to new modes of being).
Gender, on the other hand, has two senses of the word. The first refers to a universal sense of differentiation, which predates the sexed distinction. Think of the term “engendering”, wherein a thing begets another thing. Or we can also consider it as “genus” in a spiritual sense. Just as in biology, the genus Felis differentiates into species (lions, tigers), in spiritual evolution, the primal human archetype differentiates into various genders, or genera of life (avian, mammalian, etc.) before the human being itself crystallizes a distinct spiritualized entity. Thus, man engenders the animal kingdom. Differentiation is a universal principle. Likewise, the later male/female split within humanity is a higher, more conscious summa of this same principle, now applied to the human form for a moral and spiritual purpose.
The second sense is the one we will find most familiar. It is the two universal spiritual principles of Will (Masculine) and Imagination (Feminine), or a sense of “archetypal” differentiation. In other words, it is the polarization of the unified human archetype into the masculine and feminine principles and their subsequent cross-polarized incarnation into specialized male and female bodies. This can be considered a “sub-category” of the first sense. Human gender is the most advanced, conscious, and for us at least, spiritually significance instance of the universal sense of differentiation. While the “bird gender” separated to explore the principle of air/lightness/thought, human gender separation was for the purpose of generating self-consciousness and moral freedom.
Cultural expressions of gender are external reflections of the internal, spiritual polarity, filtered through the consciousness of a specific epoch and people. They are soul-led interpretations of the cosmic masculine/feminine principles. Atlantean cultural expression found a direct reflection of cosmic assignment. Women were the natural priestesses, or the interpreters of nature’s will. Men were the technicians and will-driven actors. In Post-Atlantean cultures (current), the awakening of the “I” and the intellectual mind saw the nigh omniscient memory capabilities of the past and direct perception of the spiritual principles fade away gradually. Cultures now interpret and conceptualize the gender polarity. Patriarchal cultures elevate the externalized principle of Will (law, conquest, abstract sky-god) to conscious ideals. This corresponds to the current epoch’s task of developing the masculine principle of conscious thought and agency. Matrifocal cultures may preserve more intuitive memory of the feminine cosmic principle. Luciferic or Ahrimanic cultures constitute a distortion of these principles. A Luciferic culture might exalt masculine will into arrogant individualism, or feminine imagination into a kind of hysteric sentimentality. An Ahrimanic culture might reduce masculine thought to a sterile materialism, and respectively feminine receptivity to passive collectivism. Any mix and match of these possibilities is possible.
With respect to practical shorthand, when encountering any given culture’s gender expression, we should first look for the underlying spiritual principles of “masculine and feminine” as they are emphasized, then contextualize this expression both historically and spiritually as an interpretation fitted to a specific culture’s particularity. Then, we must appraise its spiritual health. Does it foster a one-sided development, or does it reflect an arrogant individualism and the like? We should hold all such expressions non-absolutely, remembering that they are temporary “experiments” on the path towards the transcendence of the polarity itself, while retaining a respect for the fact of this differentiation. This perspective moves us beyond the typical “nature versus nurture” debates (and even the fence sitting nuance-mongers who say “it’s both” without providing a telos or actual account of how it’s both beyond providing a mutual conditioning), into a perspective that covers all bases. It provides us a concept of gender expression in culture where it is rightfully seen as filled with soul as an imperfect attempt to work with the cosmic principles, using human duality as a justification for spiritual advancement.
The Family Structure Each family role embodies a specific spiritual principle that mirrors cosmic processes. Hegel has stated that the “structure of family is the structure of mind” and such. I am in broad agreement, with one qualification: he doesn’t have a telos of gender and sex, and likewise cannot possibly have license to foresee the expansion of family into a family of all mankind, where all members freely recognize themselves as such.
The mother represents receptivity, the father represents activity, and the child represents the future and the incoming spiritual world. The family’s duty is to provide a healthy vessel and a morally sound environment so that this spirit can fulfill its mission. Education is considered a task of cooperating with the child’s own spiritual guidance.
The modern family structure is quite a relatively new phenomenon. In ancient times, individuality was weak. People identified with a group soul or clan consciousness. “Family” was the entire tribe, and leadership was by spirit (through initiates and priestesses). Procreation was guided by spiritual beings, and not personal choice. With the solidification of the “I” in the Post-Atlantean age, the family became centered on blood kinship and lineage. This was the emergence of the bloodline. It was also a necessary stage for the individuation of the ego. I would write a mini-thesis on the French Revolution here, but this post has gone on long enough. What I mean to emphasize is that ancestor worship and patriarchal lineages reflected a growing importance of inherited physical and soul qualities. However, seeing as our current fifth Post-Atlantean epoch (since around the 15th century) has had its task of individualization and freedom, the tight bonds of “bloodline” are loosened. The modern family, based on personal love and choice (e.g.; I choose to love this person and bring another person into the world), rather than obligation to clan is the result. The purpose of the modern nuclear family is to become a kind of school for free and moral relationships; where love between parents is a conscious spiritual act and decision, and raising children is a service to the independent spirit of the child. The foundation of the family structure is marriage, with the deepest foundation of marriage being the sanctified polarity between masculine and feminine principles, which each partner works to harmonize within themselves and between each other. Two souls are drawn together by spiritual laws in order to balance shared karma, or fulfill mutual obligations from past lives, or provide the specific conditions for each other’s further development. It is also a conscious reunion sanctioned by an institution, that is, in marriage, the two halves of male and female seek each other. Their attraction is the archetypical fulfillment of a reconstituted being. The spouse is the physical embodiment of the externalized opposite of one’s own soul. For man, he meets in his wife the externalized form of will, which is the tool he must learn to spiritualize. She is his power, and his living connection to active power in the world. For the woman, she meets in her husband the externalized form of internalized Imagination, which is the tool she must learn to spiritualize. He is her “source” and her living connection to a structured consciousness. Through the loving, conscious interplay of this polarity, each partner helps the either redeem and integrate their own inner opposite. The husband, through his wife, learns to imbue his thinking with warm, moral will. The wife, through her husband, learns to direct her formative forces with clarity. Together, they enact the creative act on a human scale. Marriage can be taken to be the highest human art. It is the most demanding but most rewarding spiritual practice accessible to most.
Homosexuality and Transgenderism Any critical look at homosexuality should grasp the more ideal principles from which the idea generates itself. This includes moving beyond the “telos” of natural immediacy (which often times is just biological determinism). What this implies for homosexuality, is that it can be defined as a karmically significant asymmetry between the spiritual and the physical. Normatively, it is positioned as an incarnational discord that requires resolution. The structure is determined following the eternal human spirit (which bears a qualitative bias toward either the masculine or feminine principles) which incarnates into a physical sexed body. Homosexuality then arises when this spiritual being seeks to reconcile its internal polarity outside the prescribed biological normativity, attracting toward the same sex as a mirror of its own spiritual gender. Structurally, this constitutes a bypassing of the intended domain of duality, where the male-female reproductive duality is the normative vehicle for soul evolution in the current epoch. Teleologically, it is therefore classified as a transitory developmental phenomenon, or as a suboptimal configuration that appears as humanity refines its engagement with polarity. This must eventually and ultimately be overcome as the spirit progresses toward the destined state of conscious, spiritual androgyny. Procreative duality is the intended path for human evolution. Yes, this means “marriage” is between man and woman. Same-sex attraction is spiritually meaningful, but ultimately a provisional detour.
“Transgenderism” is also positioned here as a meaningful karmic configuration. What might be diagnosed as gender dysphoria, or gender identity disorder, is interpreted as a direct carry-over of soul memory from a previous incarnation. There is the possibility here that it could stand as the remembrance of the soul of its androgynous period of time, however, various studies which find that children recalling a past-life personality of the opposite sex exhibited significantly higher rates of gender non-conformity. Look to the work of Ian Stevenson and the University of Virginia Division of Perceptual studies here. Empirically, this supports a remise that the soul’s history directly shapes present-life identity, which is much more in line with an anthroposophical outlook, than the dogmatic assertion that the soul is reverting to androgyny. For that reason, a persistent transgender identification is normatively determined as the soul’s conscious (or subconscious) retention of its previous incarnation’s gendered experience and spiritual orientation. This presents a configured mismatch- a soul is strongly imprinted with, for example, masculine spiritual attributes through numerous male lifetimes incarnates into a female body, leading to an innate sense of alienation from the physical instrument, and a compelled reinterpretation of cultural gender roles. We do not have time to go too in-depth on spiritual economy, and the principles of reincarnation, but what I mean to convey with this scenario is that the condition in the abstract is a deliberate karmic task. The soul, among other things, must consciously reconcile its enduring internal identity with the contrary spiritual lessons and social expectations of its new biological form, working toward a higher unity that advances both persona evolution, and humanity’s particular (which is also its universal) movement beyond a fixed physical polarity.
Now, I emphasize in the “abstract”, because we don’t live in the abstract, in itself world. There are influences which nudge one towards one direction or another. I mean to say that it is not a coincidence that we see the rise of transgender and homosexual advocacy at the same time as the dominance of propaganda structures, marketing firms, cosmetic and pharmaceutical industries, and so on and so forth. The mutual emergence of these things is a manifestation of Ahrimanic and Luciferic influences working upon and through these authentic soul conditions. Luciferic and Ahrimanic forces do not create these identities from nothing, they do not have the capability to truly “create”, as they are one side of the “duality” of creation (this is very vague, perhaps I will write about it in a future article). These forces actively amplify, distort, and instrumentalize in that order, for their own ends, pulling them away from their potential spiritual purposes. Luciferic influences operate through propaganda, marketing, and “digital media” (which takes its form via the internet in this era, but in future eras… perhaps I have said too much…) to inflate identity into the rebellious, absolutist ideology of the ego, or of subjectivism. It glorifies the individual’s experience into a supreme, uncompromising truth (known as Luciferic pride), cleaving it from any connection to biological reality, or higher karmic contexts, or even collective spiritual development. What it encourages is a hysterical self-creation wherein it says “you, the subject, the only subject, can be anything!”. There is a divorce of identity from connection to cosmic principles, and subsequently duty. Likewise, all traditional structures are seen as nothing but oppressive illusions. The various isms and identities one sees online and in real life, especially through the second-order medium of gender ideology (I am asexual, I am pansexual, I am a “MAP”. Although it must be stated, this Luciferic influence is not limited to gender ideology, it also exists in philosophy, particularly in moral designations. “I am a utilitarian, I am a deontologist, I am a virtue ethicist”, and so on and so forth). The Ahrimanic influence operates through the pharmaceutical and the surgical through the bureaucratic-corporate state apparatus to mechanize, commodify, and ultimately reduce these identities. It aims to frame the higher soul condition of gender dysphoria as a straightforward biochemical or surgical engineering problem to be solved by hormone blockers, surgery, and a lifelong dependence on pharmaceutical dependence. The transformative challenge and spirituality is siphoned out of the experience, becoming packaged into the sterile diagnostic codes of the DSM, profit-driven medicalization, etc.
The current landscape presents quite the corrosive synergy between these imbalance forces. However, it is good to keep in mind that one should approach homosexuality and transgenderism with a compassionate detachment, recognizing them as meaningful configurations of the soul, albeit ones that do not stand for norms. There is a challenge involved in these identities, since they can be considered soul-level conditions arising from the chosen tasks of the individual. The appropriate feeling should neither be endorsement nor condemnation, it should be a solemn recognition of a difficult spiritual curriculum being undertaken. For the transgender individual, it is the path of reconciliation. For the homosexual, it is the path of navigating polarity and attraction outside prescribed biological standard. One must respect the individual’s freedom and inner experience as a manifestation of their soul’s journey. Persecution, mockery, or coercion are grave spiritual harms that interfere not only with their karmic lessons, but with your own, and eat away at your own moral-objective being. The ideal is to provide a space of understanding which acknowledges the reality of these conditions. However, in the real world, this is much easier said than done. Concretely, these soul conditions are amplified and distorted by spiritual forces, and so, one must cultivate discernment and active moral resistance to these distortions (and hence not the abstract condition of homosexuality or transgenderism), while still upholding the core principle of compassion for the individual. One should feel not towards the person, but towards the conditions of cultural and systemic forces that encourage the exploitation and distortion of these authentic spiritual conditions. So a rejection of the Luciferic inflation involves the rejection of upholding personal identity as an absolutist ideology separate from the world and reality, and likewise a rejection of the Ahrimanic reduction involves a rejection of attitudes which sterilize and commercialize the spiritual condition into a medical or technical problem (technical problem also includes the technical problem of logistics in the commercialization of homosexuality and transgenderism). One should feel a resistance to the spirit that seeks to “solve” the dissonance through drugs and surgery, which in actuality embeds human identity within a system of materialistic control/profit. All in all, don’t hate the player, hate the game.
Post Script on Masturbation As regards the topic of masturbation, a friend asked me my thoughts on this topic. Allow me to be lazy and cite myself in a response:
It’s just one sided sexual activity. Ontologically, Kant makes the argument that sex outside of a monogamous marriage leads to objectification. In the case of masturbation, the (to be) loved person is reduced to a mere object of appetite. The conduct of a well adjusted individual would run counter to this since implicit in the sex act is the immediate presence of another that’s acknowledged on the level of personhood. You wouldn’t have sex with an animal, or some other thing that can’t consent (if you’re well adjusted). People want to have sex with another that wants to have sex with them. Yet the structure of masturbation is just this- once this desire is fulfilled, they are to be discarded with no further consideration since they have served their purpose. This tends to eliminate any moral obligation towards the other person, as they are seen as nothing more than a thing to be used and disposed of. There is another aspect of objectification that Kant mentions, and it involves the folly of one-sided determination. As you are objectifying the “actor” or “actress” on screen, you are turning yourself into an object. You turn yourself into a mere tool to satiate appetite. This connects more to the Categorical Imperative (which if you think about it is just the Law of Non-Contradiction) stuff since to turn them into means for your own ends is to destroy the structure of you in terms of your ethical obligations, as well as destroying the social fabric connecting you to other people. Moreover, Kant says that masturbation is worse than the horror of killing yourself (a bit dramatic but founded) as it debases you below the status of a beast, involving both self-destruction and surrendering to animal instincts. Below an animal, since a beast cannot debase itself into its animalistic impulses, as it already exists within an animalistic state. Cosmologically, there are a few ways to look at it. The first is in agreement with the institutional sciences of today. Practice can have negative effects: it can reinforce maladaptive neural reward pathways, contributes to sexual dysfunction, distort expectations through pornography, or interfere with emotional regulation and/or intimate relationships. However, what the sciences of today (neurology, psychology, social sciences) will not do, or rather can’t do, is inform one on the spiritual/moral normativity of such an act. In order to do this, the unbiased teacher would firstly begin with an exposition on sexual energy- what it is, it’s telos (start, end, and developmental paths), and results based off actions one would take. This can take the form of describing Kundalini, Prana, Ojas, etc. from the Vedic tradition, or Vril, which is just another name for a kind of spiritual energy (originating from Edward Bulwer-Lytton’s novel, he is the guy who is known for the quote “It was a dark and stormy night” [purple prose]), and then go into what concretely masturbation does. For example, Stylianos Atteshlis describes the unintended creation of “negative elementals” by improper use of sexual energy. Franz Bardon warns that misused sexual energy can create entities in the astral/etheric plane (if these words mean nothing to you, just read it as “the domain beyond the physical containing the psychological, emotional, etc.”) that are harmful. In addition to the negative stances and identification of what masturbation leads to, there is the positive stance of nofap and other such movements. Those are all fine and provide various benefits. However, I’m not an unbiased teacher and in addition to affirming some if not all of these frameworks, and in an effort to not write a book in general chat, I will end off by saying it’s better to not do it and to direct your attention elsewhere as an alternative outlet.
Regarding prostitution, or what people call “sex work” in order to make it more acceptable, it should be opposed in every way, shape, and form. If one wants to be “promiscuous”, they are free to do so. In fact, historically, there is quite the variety of “sexual expression” throughout the different cultures. But the promotion of transaction-based sexual energy is a black magic ritual. To the extent that this attitude of promiscuity escapes the current era of market society, it may find its place as a kind of performance art.
On Sexual Energy Manipulation As mentioned above, before the split in the ancient Lemurian epoch and prior, humanity existed in a unified androgynous spiritual form. Procreation was an immaculate process guided directly by the divine hierarchies (the Elohim, or the Spirit of Form, Jehovah, working through the Moon forces). The generative force existed, but not as we understand it. It was certainly not “sexual energy” in a polarized male-female sense. It existed as a pure, creative force flowing unconsciously through the unified human being used for virginal fructification, where the spirit would incarnate without physical intercourse. At this stage, there was no possibility or need for its manipulation by the human ego, or “I”. It was the division into sexes that liberated spiritual forces, which were previously used for bodily creation. This was the “price of thought”, as Steiner puts it. However, it also created a duality, and a descent into matter. The force became polarized and “corrupted”, seeing as the once unified creative force became bound to the newly formed material/physical male and female bodies. It descended into the “generative substances” and biochemical processes (hormones, the nervous system). It also became linked with desire via sensual pleasure and passion- look into Tree of Knowledge. This force was now in a bound, or fallen, state which served physical generation but trapped the spirit in matter. The conscious task of humanity is now to redeem this force, and return it to its higher, conscious purpose. Eventually we will transcend “manipulation”, but as of now, our task is in unifying this duality.
To clarify, this duality is not merely about behavior, it concerns the direction of consciousness and life force. There is the “downward path’ (the path of generation/dissipation) which has as it’s core principle outflow and externalization. Consciousness follows the force of desire outward, seeking union and satisfaction, and ultimately seeking creation in the external, physical world. The creative impulse which was born in the astral body as light and energy, is immediately channeled downward through the glandular and nervous systems. Becoming bound in the biochemical processes, it is expended in physical procreation, or the intense emotional charges of desire, passion, and personal attachment (so, including but not limited to masturbation, or other spiritual practices which we will get to shortly). As a result, the energy is “fixed” into matter, becoming a kind of animal sacrifice. The astral body is imprinted with passionate impressions (the sensory memories, attachments, and desires of these experiences) which creates a karmic content that must later be purified. While it serves the purpose of Earthly destiny and propels reincarnation, the other side of the bargain understands this as reinforcing identification with the physical self and thickens the veil separating us from the spiritual world. The symbol for this “downward path” is the Moon. It fits perfectly, in my opinion, since it reflects external light (the desire for outer objects), governs the cyclical biological rhythms of menstruation and fertility, and acts as the receptive principle (in many senses, but the most relevant sense is that it receives the sun). The “upward path”, or the path of regeneration/integration, on the other hand, has the opposite polarity/principle. It follows inflow and internalization and uses will to withdraw the force from external objects and redirect it inward, seeking union and creation within the internal, spiritual being. Through conscious resistance to immediate gratification (such as via the act of sacrifice), the same astral impulse is halted in its downward course. To put it another way, by “holding the seed” it is conserved. This is done through meditation, moral idealism, artistic creation, or various other techniques, the dammed up force is (to borrow a term from alchemy) sublimated. It is guided upward, and re-absorbed into the nervous system, resulting in a direction to nourish the brain and spiritual centers (or chakras). The energy here is redeemed, since it ceases to be generative (in the sense of procreating physical children) and becomes the procreative spiritual consciousness of the “Virgin Sophia”. Of course, the symbol here is the Sun. The sun emanates its own light and signifies the active, masculine principle. Both paths are perfectly depicted in the ancient emblem of the Caduceus of Hermes. The central staff is the human spine (sushumna), with the two intertwining serpents representing the downward, lunar, feminine, and upward, solar, masculine currents of force. Their crossing points are the spiritual centers, or chakras. The task of the initiate is to balance and unite these two “serpents” at each level, culminating in the wings at the crown- the liberation of the spirit.
The symbol of the centaur also provides us with a very potent archetypal image for the concepts of the upward and downward spiritual paths. It represents the dual nature within every person, with you as the centaur. The higher spiritual consciousness, or the human rider, is tasked with guiding and mastering the lower “astral beast”, or the animalistic realm of drives and passions and instincts which forms the lower part of your “soul” (the astral body). This inner struggle directly corresponds to the choice between the two paths of downward, following desires into physical expression, or the upward path of sacrifice and redirection of the same energy inward for spiritual regeneration. The reason this maps so well as a symbol is because it reiterates the actual stage of human development during the Lemurian epoch, where our ancestry were half animal and half spiritual beings. This was before the separation of the sexes, and the full emergence of physical sexuality in the middle of the Lemurian. Hence, the centaur is the primordial human condition from which the powerful source of sexual energy later differentiated and became something that required conscious redemption and mastery over.
Many traditions have latched onto this understanding of “generative”, or sexual energy. I will go over a few from certain traditions as a cross-cultural validation for the understanding provided above, for the reason that they all describe the same universal principles of life force polarity, and transformation, albeit using different cultural languages.
Prana is the universal vital force in Yoga, representing the omnipresent raw material. It is the “breath of life” which animates both the unified pre-split human, and the polarized being of today. Vril, from 19th century occultism, most closely resembles the operational force of regeneration as a tangible, and utilizable, etheric power that results from an inner alchemy. It functions as the battery for spiritual faculties. Because my website has the yin-yang symbol on it, I will go a bit more in depth on this aspect. The concept of Yin and Yang from Taoism furnishes for us the fundamental law that underlies spiritual reality, as the description of the interpenetrating process of transformation itself. Yin (receptive, lunar, contracting) and Yang (active, solar, expanding) are interdependent phases in one continuous cycle, each containing the seed of the other, perpetually giving way to one another like a cosmic breath (breath is used a lot, one can read “breath” in most contexts as “spirit”). The “downward path” is best understood as the phase where Yang turns toward Yin, as a description of active force condensing and becoming receptive to manifestation. The Upward path is the opposite, where Yin is transformed into Yang, through receptive consolidation and inner alchemy. The energy is purified and catalyzed to release its latent spiritual potential upward. This process of conscious transformation is the explicit focus of the Taoist internal alchemy known as Neidan, which seeks to unify both within the human body-microcosm, to generate the immortal “golden elixir”. This work operates through the three treasures of Jing, Qi, and Shen.
In order to avoid this becoming a Daoist metaphysics article, it is best to stop there. The spiritual “paths” described center on the transformation of the foundational forces found within human nature. Such work begins with the understanding that man is a merger of a higher spiritual part, and a lower, animalistic astral part which houses our drives and passions, including our sexuality. The core task should not be confused with a repression or suppression of these forces, since this kind of activity will eventually burst from within, in one way or another. Instead, one should seek the conscious transformation of sexual energy through the “I” or higher consciousness. As such, sexual energy in this view is a form through which deeper spiritual forces manifest.
Feminism Modern “feminism’s” development is inextricably tied to the logic of capitalism/modernity. This alliance, has from the very onset, influenced the meaning of the liberation of woman, commodifying it into a product to be acquired. Freedom can no longer be framed as collective emancipation from “patriarchal” structures, instead being transformed into the individual’s capacity for increased participation in the labor market, and the expansion of personal consumerist choice. Of course, the aspirational icon is also transformed, becoming the “financially independent” woman whose power is stamped by her salary and purchasing ability. In this paradigm, the struggle shifts from eradicating oppression - and I mean true oppression - into securing a more equitable distribution of individuals within the existing hierarchy of the marketplace. Some may argue that feminism was not always this blatantly Mammonic, however I will address this soon. The primary critique of this form of feminism is understood to be that “oppression” against the fairer sex is not actually a goal. Instead, it is alignment with economic power and achieving a professional status within the established network of power. The cry for a “seat at the table” concedes the table’s existence and legitimizes it, focusing effort on a kind of inclusion within social institutions and civic society. The ultimate outcome is a version of feminism that creates the “girlboss” archetype; the ambitious, resilient, self-optimizing, perpetually productive worker. She is encouraged to bypass structural critiques in favor of John Henryist grind-culture, seeing her exhaustion as a badge of honor, or in some cases, using work as a therapeutic outlet for deeper problems we will soon discuss.
Modern feminism, being shaped by a capitalist/modernist framework, tacitly accepts “masculinity” as the dominant and legitimate mode of interfacing with the world. The values of this mode, abstract rationality, competitive ambition, compartmentalization, the will to dominate and transform external matters, and so on and so forth, are treated as the default standard for success and personhood, instead of one polarity of human potential. In order to be recognized as full participants in civil society, women are implicitly encouraged to adopt this attitude. What results is an assimilation where the worth of a woman’s contribution is measured by how adeptly she performs a masculinized script of disengagement and production. Likewise, the existential dimensions historically associated with womanhood are systematically erased or devalued. Roles like mother, grandmother, and daughter (with this last one having overlap with the section on transgenderism) which encompass relationships beyond biology, into continuity, sacrifice, etc. are relegated to the private sphere as a one among many “personal choice” instead of being honored as culturally and spiritually central vocations. Deeper feminine principles are dismissed as unproductive passivity or sentimentalism. The most tragic aspect of modernity’s erasure of womanhood is not that it elevates women into a true, integrated masculinity, but that it pressures them to perform a bastardized caricature of it. What is presented as “empowerment” is often the adoption of masculinity’s most external and transactional aspects, stripped of all the virtues which traditionally gave masculinity its purpose in the first place. It is unsustainable. This is also a negative aspect for men as well, since men are forced to “erase the feminine”, so to say, in emphasizing the one-sided aspects of masculinity. We can consider “traditional” masculinity (drawing upon an ideal development, in contrast to its current negative manifestation. I don’t mean to advocate for “traditionalism”) in its archetypal understanding, as oriented towards mastery as service. The will to power was channeled through sacrifice or stewardship. The knight or equivalent figure protected the weak, the craftsman honored his materials, for example. The drive becomes tempered by a discipline and connection to the world beyond the self. The “girlboss” model, by contrast, promotes a necrotic puppet of capitalist-compatible pseudo-masculinity. Seizing the instruments of masculine agency, it severs these from any connection to a higher ethic or end.
How does this make sense? How can one seek mastery of the world but end up turning the self into an object to be mastered? How can one embrace the aggression of masculinity, but abandon the responsibility that prompted and results from it? Well, let’s be more concrete and talk about personhood. It makes perfect sense when one considers that the logical endpoint of modernity as a society which reduces all value to an abstract market logic. The same logic which produced the logic of masculinity in the girlboss was the kernel present in the foundational feminism of early America, specifically paralleling the antiblackness within mainstream feminism. Feminism in all current forms (so no alternative “womanist” conceptions unfortuantely) is structurally and philosophically antagonistic to “Black” life, particularly the black male life, as a function of this inverted coopting of masculinity. The white, bourgeois subject’s liberation is conceptualized in opposition to, or through the symbolic use of, black subjugation.
